root_text.an4.197:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an4.197:1.2: Atha kho mallikā devī yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho mallikā devī bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.197:2.1: “Ko nu kho, bhante, hetu ko paccayo, yena midhekacco mātugāmo dubbaṇṇā ca hoti durūpā supāpikā dassanāya;
root_text.an4.197:2.2: daliddā ca hoti appassakā appabhogā appesakkhā ca?
root_text.an4.197:3.1: Ko pana, bhante, hetu ko paccayo, yena midhekacco mātugāmo dubbaṇṇā ca hoti durūpā supāpikā dassanāya;
root_text.an4.197:3.2: aḍḍhā ca hoti mahaddhanā mahābhogā mahesakkhā ca?
root_text.an4.197:4.1: Ko nu kho, bhante, hetu ko paccayo, yena midhekacco mātugāmo abhirūpā ca hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā paramāya vaṇṇapokkharatāya samannāgatā;
root_text.an4.197:4.2: daliddā ca hoti appassakā appabhogā appesakkhā ca?
root_text.an4.197:5.1: Ko pana, bhante, hetu ko paccayo, yena midhekacco mātugāmo abhirūpā ca hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā paramāya vaṇṇapokkharatāya samannāgatā, aḍḍhā ca hoti mahaddhanā mahābhogā mahesakkhā cā”ti?
root_text.an4.197:6.1: “Idha, mallike, ekacco mātugāmo kodhanā hoti upāyāsabahulā.
root_text.an4.197:6.2: Appampi vuttā samānā abhisajjati kuppati byāpajjati patitthīyati, kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti.
root_text.an4.197:6.3: Sā na dātā hoti samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.197:6.6: Sā ce tato cutā itthattaṁ āgacchati, sā yattha yattha paccājāyati dubbaṇṇā ca hoti durūpā supāpikā dassanāya;
root_text.an4.197:6.7: daliddā ca hoti appassakā appabhogā appesakkhā ca.
root_text.an4.197:7.1: Idha pana, mallike, ekacco mātugāmo kodhanā hoti upāyāsabahulā.
root_text.an4.197:7.2: Appampi vuttā samānā abhisajjati kuppati byāpajjati patitthīyati, kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti.
root_text.an4.197:7.3: Sā dātā hoti samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.197:7.5: paralābhasakkāragarukāramānanavandanapūjanāsu na issati na upadussati na issaṁ bandhati.
root_text.an4.197:7.6: Sā ce tato cutā itthattaṁ āgacchati, sā yattha yattha paccājāyati dubbaṇṇā ca hoti durūpā supāpikā dassanāya;
root_text.an4.197:7.7: aḍḍhā ca hoti mahaddhanā mahābhogā mahesakkhā ca.
root_text.an4.197:8.1: Idha pana, mallike, ekacco mātugāmo akkodhanā hoti anupāyāsabahulā.
root_text.an4.197:8.2: Bahumpi vuttā samānā nābhisajjati na kuppati na byāpajjati na patitthīyati, na kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti.
root_text.an4.197:8.3: Sā na dātā hoti samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.197:8.6: Sā ce tato cutā itthattaṁ āgacchati, sā yattha yattha paccājāyati abhirūpā ca hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā paramāya vaṇṇapokkharatāya samannāgatā;
root_text.an4.197:8.7: daliddā ca hoti appassakā appabhogā appesakkhā ca.
root_text.an4.197:9.1: Idha pana, mallike, ekacco mātugāmo akkodhanā hoti anupāyāsabahulā.
root_text.an4.197:9.2: Bahumpi vuttā samānā nābhisajjati na kuppati na byāpajjati na patitthīyati, na kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti.
root_text.an4.197:9.3: Sā dātā hoti samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.197:9.5: paralābhasakkāragarukāramānanavandanapūjanāsu na issati na upadussati na issaṁ bandhati.
root_text.an4.197:9.6: Sā ce tato cutā itthattaṁ āgacchati, sā yattha yattha paccājāyati abhirūpā ca hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā paramāya vaṇṇapokkharatāya samannāgatā;
root_text.an4.197:9.7: aḍḍhā ca hoti mahaddhanā mahābhogā mahesakkhā ca.
root_text.an4.197:10.1: Ayaṁ kho, mallike, hetu ayaṁ paccayo, yena midhekacco mātugāmo dubbaṇṇā ca hoti durūpā supāpikā dassanāya;
root_text.an4.197:10.2: daliddā ca hoti appassakā appabhogā appesakkhā ca.
root_text.an4.197:10.3: Ayaṁ pana, mallike, hetu ayaṁ paccayo, yena midhekacco mātugāmo dubbaṇṇā ca hoti durūpā supāpikā dassanāya;
root_text.an4.197:10.4: aḍḍhā ca hoti mahaddhanā mahābhogā mahesakkhā ca.
root_text.an4.197:10.5: Ayaṁ kho, mallike, hetu ayaṁ paccayo, yena midhekacco mātugāmo abhirūpā ca hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā paramāya vaṇṇapokkharatāya samannāgatā;
root_text.an4.197:10.6: daliddā ca hoti appassakā appabhogā appesakkhā ca.
root_text.an4.197:10.7: Ayaṁ pana, mallike, hetu ayaṁ paccayo, yena midhekacco mātugāmo abhirūpā ca hoti dassanīyā pāsādikā paramāya vaṇṇapokkharatāya samannāgatā;
root_text.an4.197:10.8: aḍḍhā ca hoti mahaddhanā mahābhogā mahesakkhā cā”ti.
root_text.an4.197:11.1: Evaṁ vutte, mallikā devī bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.197:11.2: “yā nūnāhaṁ, bhante, aññaṁ jātiṁ kodhanā ahosiṁ upāyāsabahulā,
root_text.an4.197:11.3: appampi vuttā samānā abhisajjiṁ kuppiṁ byāpajjiṁ patitthīyiṁ kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātvākāsiṁ,
root_text.an4.197:11.4: sāhaṁ, bhante, etarahi dubbaṇṇā durūpā supāpikā dassanāya.
root_text.an4.197:12.1: Yā nūnāhaṁ, bhante, aññaṁ jātiṁ dātā ahosiṁ samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ, sāhaṁ, bhante, etarahi aḍḍhā mahaddhanā mahābhogā.
root_text.an4.197:13.1: Yā nūnāhaṁ, bhante, aññaṁ jātiṁ anissāmanikā ahosiṁ, paralābhasakkāragarukāramānanavandanapūjanāsu na issiṁ na upadussiṁ na issaṁ bandhiṁ, sāhaṁ, bhante, etarahi mahesakkhā.
root_text.an4.197:13.2: Santi kho pana, bhante, imasmiṁ rājakule khattiyakaññāpi brāhmaṇakaññāpi gahapatikaññāpi, tāsāhaṁ issarādhipaccaṁ kāremi.
root_text.an4.197:13.3: Esāhaṁ, bhante, ajjatagge akkodhanā bhavissāmi anupāyāsabahulā,
root_text.an4.197:13.4: bahumpi vuttā samānā nābhisajjissāmi na kuppissāmi na byāpajjissāmi na patitthīyissāmi, kopañca dosañca appaccayañca na pātukarissāmi;
root_text.an4.197:13.5: dassāmi samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ.
root_text.an4.197:13.6: Anissāmanikā bhavissāmi, paralābhasakkāragarukāramānanavandanapūjanāsu na ississāmi na upadussissāmi na issaṁ bandhissāmi.
root_text.an4.197:13.7: Abhikkantaṁ, bhante …pe… upāsikaṁ maṁ, bhante, bhagavā dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.an4.197:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an4.197:1.2: Then Queen Mallikā went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an4.197:2.1: “What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why in this life some ladies are ugly, unattractive, and bad-looking;
translation_text.an4.197:2.2: and poor, with few assets and possessions; and insignificant?
translation_text.an4.197:3.1: And why are some ladies ugly, unattractive, and bad-looking;
translation_text.an4.197:3.2: but rich, affluent, wealthy, and illustrious?
translation_text.an4.197:4.1: And why are some ladies attractive, good-looking, lovely, of surpassing beauty;
translation_text.an4.197:4.2: but poor, with few assets and possessions; and insignificant?
translation_text.an4.197:5.1: And why are some ladies attractive, good-looking, lovely, of surpassing beauty; and rich, affluent, wealthy, and illustrious?”
translation_text.an4.197:6.1: “Take a lady who is irritable and bad-tempered.
translation_text.an4.197:6.2: Even when criticized a little bit she loses her temper, becoming annoyed, hostile, and hard-hearted, and displaying annoyance, hate, and bitterness.
translation_text.an4.197:6.3: She doesn’t give to ascetics or brahmins such things as food, drink, clothing, vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.an4.197:6.5: envying, resenting, and begrudging the possessions, honor, respect, reverence, homage, and veneration given to others.
translation_text.an4.197:6.6: If she comes back to this place after passing away, wherever she is reborn she’s ugly, unattractive, and bad-looking;
translation_text.an4.197:6.7: and poor, with few assets and possessions; and insignificant.
translation_text.an4.197:7.1: Take another lady who is irritable and bad-tempered. …
translation_text.an4.197:7.3: But she does give to ascetics or brahmins …
translation_text.an4.197:7.6: If she comes back to this place after passing away, wherever she is reborn she’s ugly, unattractive, and bad-looking;
translation_text.an4.197:7.7: but rich, affluent, wealthy, and illustrious.
translation_text.an4.197:8.1: Take another lady who isn’t irritable and bad-tempered. …
translation_text.an4.197:8.3: But she doesn’t give to ascetics or brahmins …
translation_text.an4.197:8.6: If she comes back to this place after passing away, wherever she is reborn she’s attractive, good-looking, lovely, of surpassing beauty;
translation_text.an4.197:8.7: but poor, with few assets and possessions; and insignificant.
translation_text.an4.197:9.1: Take another lady who isn’t irritable and bad-tempered. …
translation_text.an4.197:9.3: She gives to ascetics and brahmins …
translation_text.an4.197:9.6: If she comes back to this place after passing away, wherever she is reborn she’s attractive, good-looking, lovely, of surpassing beauty;
translation_text.an4.197:9.7: and rich, affluent, wealthy, and illustrious.
translation_text.an4.197:10.1: This is why some ladies are ugly …
translation_text.an4.197:11.1: When this was said, Queen Mallikā said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an4.197:11.2: “Sir, in another life I must have been irritable and bad-tempered.
translation_text.an4.197:11.3: Even when lightly criticized I must have lost my temper, becoming annoyed, hostile, and hard-hearted, and displaying annoyance, hate, and bitterness.
translation_text.an4.197:11.4: For now I am ugly, unattractive, and bad-looking.
translation_text.an4.197:12.1: In another life I must have given to ascetics or brahmins such things as food, drink, clothing, vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting. For now I am rich, affluent, and wealthy.
translation_text.an4.197:13.1: In another life, I must not have been jealous, envying, resenting, and begrudging the possessions, honor, respect, reverence, homage, and veneration given to others. For now I am illustrious.
translation_text.an4.197:13.2: In this royal court I command maidens of the aristocrats, brahmins, and householders.
translation_text.an4.197:13.3: So, sir, from this day forth I will not be irritable and bad-tempered.
translation_text.an4.197:13.4: Even when heavily criticized I won’t lose my temper, become annoyed, hostile, and hard-hearted, or display annoyance, hate, and bitterness.
translation_text.an4.197:13.5: I will give to ascetics or brahmins such things as food, drink, clothing, vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.an4.197:13.6: I will not be jealous, envying, resenting, and begrudging the possessions, honor, respect, reverence, homage, and veneration given to others.
translation_text.an4.197:13.7: Excellent, sir! … From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.an4.197:11.2: nūnāhaṁ → sā nūnāhaṁ (sya-all); yaṁ nūnāhaṁ (pts1ed, mr) | aññaṁ jātiṁ → aññāya jātiyā (sya-all)
